LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-COMPLAINANTS PHOENIX ARTS ASSOCIATION, LINDA AYRES-FREDERICK, AND TY MCKENZIE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING- GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. CROSS-DEFENDANT ALEJANDRO TORRES' OBJECTIONS TO THE EVIDENCE PROVIDED IN SUPPORT OF THE CROSS-COMPLAINANTS' MOTION ARE ALL OVERRULED. EACH PIECE OF EVIDENCE MR. TORRES OBJECTED TO IS RELEVANT. MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Notice Of Defendant Alejandro Torres Motion For Leave Of Court To File Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-30-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Thomas W.J. Purtell, of The Law Offices of Thomas W.J. Purtell, Tel. 415.722.6291, for defendants Alejandro Torres;
Paulo S. Kline Simon, of Clapp Moroney Vucinich Beeman Scheley, Tel. 925.734.0990, for defendants Phoenix Arts Association, Linda Ayres-Frederick & Ty McKenzie;
Lisa A. Corman, of Lilac Law Group, LLC, Tel. 415.967.2551, for defendants Megan Hardy, appearing via CourtCall.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ALEJANDRO TORRES' MOTION FOR LEAVE Of Court To File Cross-Complaint is denied. The motion fails to state when the facts giving rise to the proposed cross-complaint were discovered or why this motion was not made earlier. Moreover, the motion fails to address the arguments raised in opposition to the motion that granting the motion is futile because all of the cross-claims lack merit as a matter of law. (Weil and Brown book at 6:645-6:646.1 citing case law denying leave to amend where a proposed new claim clearly lacks merit). Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-31-2016 Set For Trial On Apr-17-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Msj
Off Calendar Dec-27-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Cross-Complainants Phoenix Arts Association, Linda Ayres-Frederick, and Ty McKenzie's Motion for Summary Adjudication is GRANTED IN PART and DENIED IN PART. As to the issue of the duty to defend, cross-complainants' motion for summary adjudication is GRANTED. The duty to defend is broad and arises when the claim alleges "facts which would give rise to a duty of indemnity." (Crawford v. Weather Shield Mfg. Inc. (2008) 44 Cal.4th 541, 558.) The plaintiff's complaint alleges premises liability and negligence against all defendants and the indemnity provision is written broadly enough to encompass such claims. (Compendium of Evidence, Ex. 1; Ex. 4.) As to the duty to indemnify, cross-complainants' motion for summary adjudication is DENIED. The court finds ruling on the issue of the duty to indemnify is inappropriate at this stage because the cross-complainants have not yet incurred liability or paid claims subject to indemnity. As to the duty to reimburse for attorney's fees, cross-complainants' motion for summary adjudication is DENIED. The court finds it is inappropriate to rule on the issue of the duty to reimburse at this stage. Furthermore, cross-complainants have not met their summary adjudication burden because they have not separately addressed the duty to reimburse in their memorandum of points and authorities. The duty to reimburse is distinct from the duty to defend. (Crawford, supra, 44 Cal.4th at 564.) Cross-Defendant Alejandro Torres' objections to the evidence provided in support of the cross-complainants' motion are all overruled. Each piece of evidence Mr. Torres objected to is relevant. Order signed. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ru
